privacy: Your files are processed locally in your browser. Nothing is uploaded to any server.

convertMp4ToGifDesc: Convert MP4 video clips to animated GIF online for free. Create shareable GIFs from any video. Custom frame rate and size.

mergePhotos: Merge Photos Online - Free Image Combiner

extractMp3: Extract MP3 from Video Online - Free

convertJpgToPngHowTo: How to Convert JPG to PNG
convertJpgToPngStep1Desc: Drag and drop or click to select your JPG/JPEG image file from your device.
convertJpgToPngStep2Desc: Select PNG as the output format and click Start to begin the conversion process.
convertJpgToPngStep3Desc: Your converted PNG file is ready. Download it instantly to your device.
convertJpgToPngFaq1Q: Does converting JPG to PNG improve image quality?
convertJpgToPngFaq1A: Converting JPG to PNG does not recover quality lost during JPG compression. However, PNG uses lossless compression, so your image will not lose any additional quality on future saves. This makes PNG ideal for images you plan to edit repeatedly.
convertJpgToPngFaq2Q: Why is the PNG file larger than the original JPG?
convertJpgToPngFaq2A: PNG uses lossless compression that preserves every pixel exactly, resulting in larger file sizes. JPG achieves smaller files by discarding image data through lossy compression. The trade-off is that PNG delivers pixel-perfect quality with transparency support.
convertJpgToPngFaq3Q: When should I use PNG instead of JPG?
convertJpgToPngFaq3A: Use PNG for images requiring transparency, logos, screenshots, text overlays, or graphics that will be edited multiple times. JPG is better suited for photographs and large images where smaller file size matters more than pixel-perfect preservation.
convertJpgToWebpHowTo: How to Convert JPG to WebP
convertJpgToWebpStep1Desc: Drag and drop or browse to select the JPG image you want to convert to WebP format.
convertJpgToWebpStep2Desc: Select WebP as the target format. Adjust quality settings if needed for optimal file size reduction.
convertJpgToWebpStep3Desc: Your optimized WebP file is ready. Download it and enjoy significantly smaller file sizes for web use.
convertJpgToWebpFaq1Q: How much smaller is WebP compared to JPG?
convertJpgToWebpFaq1A: WebP images are typically 25-35% smaller than equivalent JPG files at the same visual quality. This reduction comes from WebP's more efficient compression algorithm, making it ideal for website optimization where faster page load times improve SEO and user experience.
convertJpgToWebpFaq2Q: Do all browsers support WebP format?
convertJpgToWebpFaq2A: All modern browsers including Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge, and Opera fully support WebP. Legacy browsers like Internet Explorer do not support it. For websites, you can serve WebP with a JPG fallback using the HTML picture element to ensure universal compatibility.
convertJpgToWebpFaq3Q: Does converting JPG to WebP lose quality?
convertJpgToWebpFaq3A: WebP supports both lossy and lossless compression. With lossy WebP at equivalent quality settings, the visual difference from JPG is imperceptible to the human eye while delivering smaller file sizes. Lossless WebP preserves every pixel but produces larger files than lossy mode.
convertPngToJpgHowTo: How to Convert PNG to JPG
convertPngToJpgStep1Desc: Upload your PNG file by dragging it into the drop zone or clicking to browse your files.
convertPngToJpgStep2Desc: Choose JPG as the output format and adjust the quality slider to balance file size and image clarity.
convertPngToJpgStep3Desc: Download the converted JPG image. File sizes are typically 50-80% smaller than the original PNG.
convertPngToJpgFaq1Q: What happens to transparency when converting PNG to JPG?
convertPngToJpgFaq1A: JPG does not support transparency, so any transparent areas in your PNG will be filled with a solid background color, typically white. If you need to preserve transparency, consider converting to WebP instead, which supports both transparency and smaller file sizes.
convertPngToJpgFaq2Q: How much can I reduce the file size by converting PNG to JPG?
convertPngToJpgFaq2A: Converting PNG to JPG typically reduces file size by 50-90%, depending on image complexity and JPG quality settings. Photographs see the largest reductions because JPG's lossy compression excels at encoding photographic content with gradual color transitions.
convertPngToJpgFaq3Q: What JPG quality setting should I use?
convertPngToJpgFaq3A: For web images, 80-85% quality offers the best balance between visual fidelity and file size. For print or archival use, choose 95-100%. Below 70%, compression artifacts become noticeable, especially around sharp edges and text, so avoid low quality for images with fine details.
convertPngToWebpHowTo: How to Convert PNG to WebP
convertPngToWebpStep1Desc: Drag and drop your PNG image or click to browse. Supports all PNG files including those with transparency.
convertPngToWebpStep2Desc: Choose WebP as output. WebP preserves PNG transparency while delivering dramatically smaller file sizes.
convertPngToWebpStep3Desc: Save your converted WebP image. Enjoy up to 45% smaller files with identical visual quality.
convertPngToWebpFaq1Q: Does WebP support transparency like PNG?
convertPngToWebpFaq1A: Yes, WebP fully supports alpha channel transparency just like PNG. The key advantage is that transparent WebP images are significantly smaller than equivalent transparent PNGs, often 40-50% smaller, making them ideal for website logos, icons, and overlay graphics.
convertPngToWebpFaq2Q: Is PNG to WebP conversion lossless?
convertPngToWebpFaq2A: WebP supports both lossless and lossy modes. Lossless WebP preserves every pixel identically to the original PNG but still achieves 20-30% smaller files. Lossy WebP reduces size even further with minimal visual impact, making it perfect for web performance optimization.
convertPngToWebpFaq3Q: Should I replace all PNG images on my website with WebP?
convertPngToWebpFaq3A: For most websites, converting PNGs to WebP significantly improves page load speed and Core Web Vitals scores. Use the HTML picture element to serve WebP to modern browsers with PNG as a fallback. This approach ensures compatibility while maximizing performance benefits.
convertWebpToJpgHowTo: How to Convert WebP to JPG
convertWebpToJpgStep1Desc: Select your WebP file by dragging it in or browsing your device. All WebP files are supported.
convertWebpToJpgStep2Desc: Choose JPG as the target format and start the conversion. Processing happens instantly in your browser.
convertWebpToJpgStep3Desc: Save the converted JPG file. It is now compatible with every image viewer, editor, and platform.
convertWebpToJpgFaq1Q: Why would I convert WebP to JPG?
convertWebpToJpgFaq1A: Some older software, email clients, and social media platforms do not fully support WebP. Converting to JPG ensures your image is universally compatible with every device, operating system, and application. JPG is the most widely supported image format available today.
convertWebpToJpgFaq2Q: Will the JPG be larger than the WebP file?
convertWebpToJpgFaq2A: Usually yes, because WebP uses a more efficient compression algorithm than JPG. At equivalent visual quality, JPG files are typically 25-35% larger. You can reduce JPG file size by lowering the quality slider, though this introduces more visible compression artifacts.
convertWebpToJpgFaq3Q: Can I batch convert multiple WebP files to JPG?
convertWebpToJpgFaq3A: Yes, you can upload multiple WebP files and convert them all to JPG in one session. Each file is processed individually in your browser with no server upload required. This makes batch conversion fast, private, and free regardless of how many images you need to convert.
convertWebpToPngHowTo: How to Convert WebP to PNG
convertWebpToPngStep1Desc: Upload the WebP image you want to convert. Supports both lossy and lossless WebP files.
convertWebpToPngStep2Desc: Select PNG as output format. Any transparency in your WebP file is fully preserved in the PNG result.
convertWebpToPngStep3Desc: Your lossless PNG file is ready to download. Use it in any image editor or platform without compatibility concerns.
convertWebpToPngFaq1Q: Does converting WebP to PNG preserve transparency?
convertWebpToPngFaq1A: Yes, all transparency and alpha channel data from the original WebP file is fully preserved during PNG conversion. The resulting PNG supports the same transparency levels, making it safe to convert logos, icons, and overlay graphics without losing transparent areas.
convertWebpToPngFaq2Q: Why is PNG better than WebP for editing?
convertWebpToPngFaq2A: PNG uses lossless compression, so repeatedly opening and saving a PNG file never degrades quality. Some image editors also have limited WebP support. Converting to PNG gives you maximum compatibility with professional tools like Photoshop, GIMP, and Illustrator.
convertWebpToPngFaq3Q: Is the PNG file larger than the original WebP?
convertWebpToPngFaq3A: Yes, PNG files are typically larger because PNG compression is less efficient than WebP. However, the trade-off is universal compatibility and lossless quality. If file size is critical, keep the WebP version for web use and the PNG version for editing and archiving.
convertHeicToJpgHowTo: How to Convert HEIC to JPG
convertHeicToJpgStep1Desc: Select your iPhone or iPad HEIC/HEIF photo file. You can upload directly from your device or cloud storage.
convertHeicToJpgStep2Desc: The tool automatically converts your HEIC file to universally compatible JPG format in your browser.
convertHeicToJpgStep3Desc: Save your converted JPG photo. It can now be opened on any device, shared via email, or uploaded anywhere.
convertHeicToJpgFaq1Q: What is HEIC format and why does Apple use it?
convertHeicToJpgFaq1A: HEIC (High Efficiency Image Container) is Apple's default photo format since iOS 11. It produces files roughly 50% smaller than JPG at equivalent quality using HEVC compression. Apple adopted it to save storage space on iPhones while maintaining excellent photo quality.
convertHeicToJpgFaq2Q: Why can't Windows open HEIC files?
convertHeicToJpgFaq2A: Windows requires a separate HEVC codec extension to open HEIC files, which Microsoft charges for in the Microsoft Store. Converting HEIC to JPG eliminates this compatibility issue entirely, letting you view and share your iPhone photos on any Windows PC without installing extra software.
convertHeicToJpgFaq3Q: Does converting HEIC to JPG reduce photo quality?
convertHeicToJpgFaq3A: There is a minimal quality reduction because JPG uses lossy compression. At high quality settings like 90-95%, the visual difference is imperceptible for most photographs. The conversion trades a slight quality loss for universal compatibility across all devices, platforms, and applications.
convertBmpToJpgHowTo: How to Convert BMP to JPG
convertBmpToJpgStep1Desc: Select your BMP bitmap image. BMP files are often very large since they store uncompressed pixel data.
convertBmpToJpgStep2Desc: Choose JPG output and adjust quality. Even at maximum quality, JPG will be dramatically smaller than BMP.
convertBmpToJpgStep3Desc: Save your JPG file. Expect 90-95% file size reduction compared to the original uncompressed BMP image.
convertBmpToJpgFaq1Q: Why are BMP files so much larger than JPG?
convertBmpToJpgFaq1A: BMP stores every pixel as raw, uncompressed data with no compression algorithm applied. A 1920x1080 BMP image takes about 6MB of space. JPG applies lossy compression to reduce this dramatically, often to 200-500KB, by intelligently discarding visual information humans cannot perceive.
convertBmpToJpgFaq2Q: When would someone still use BMP format?
convertBmpToJpgFaq2A: BMP is occasionally used in legacy Windows applications, embedded systems, and medical imaging where uncompressed data is required. Some older scanner software also outputs BMP by default. For everyday use like sharing, web publishing, or email, converting BMP to JPG is strongly recommended.
convertBmpToJpgFaq3Q: Can I convert BMP to JPG without losing quality?
convertBmpToJpgFaq3A: JPG is inherently lossy, so some data is discarded during conversion. However, at 95-100% quality, the visual difference is virtually undetectable. If you need truly lossless conversion, consider PNG instead of JPG, though the file size reduction will be less dramatic.
convertMp4ToWebmHowTo: How to Convert MP4 to WebM
convertMp4ToWebmStep1Desc: Select your MP4 video file. All processing happens locally in your browser using WebAssembly-powered FFmpeg.
convertMp4ToWebmStep2Desc: Choose WebM as output format. Adjust resolution and quality settings to optimize for your web embedding needs.
convertMp4ToWebmStep3Desc: Save your converted WebM video. It is optimized for HTML5 video playback and web page embedding.
convertMp4ToWebmFaq1Q: What are the advantages of WebM over MP4?
convertMp4ToWebmFaq1A: WebM is an open, royalty-free format developed by Google, making it ideal for web use without licensing concerns. WebM files using VP9 codec deliver comparable quality to MP4 H.264 at smaller file sizes, improving page load times and reducing bandwidth costs for websites.
convertMp4ToWebmFaq2Q: Can all browsers play WebM videos?
convertMp4ToWebmFaq2A: Chrome, Firefox, Edge, and Opera fully support WebM playback. Safari added WebM support in version 14.1. For maximum compatibility, many websites serve both WebM and MP4 using the HTML5 video element with multiple source tags, letting browsers choose the format they support best.
convertMp4ToWebmFaq3Q: Does MP4 to WebM conversion take long?
convertMp4ToWebmFaq3A: Conversion time depends on video length, resolution, and your device performance. Short clips under 2 minutes typically convert in 30-60 seconds. Since processing runs entirely in your browser via WebAssembly, faster CPUs produce faster results. No video data is uploaded to any server.
convertMp4ToAviHowTo: How to Convert MP4 to AVI
convertMp4ToAviStep1Desc: Select the MP4 video you want to convert to AVI format. Supports all MP4 files with any codec.
convertMp4ToAviStep2Desc: Choose AVI as the output format. The converter handles codec transcoding automatically in your browser.
convertMp4ToAviStep3Desc: Save the converted AVI file. It is now compatible with older media players and legacy video editing software.
convertMp4ToAviFaq1Q: Why would I convert MP4 to AVI?
convertMp4ToAviFaq1A: AVI is needed for compatibility with some older video editing software, legacy media players, and specific hardware devices that do not support MP4. Certain professional video workflows and older DVD authoring tools also require AVI input format for proper processing.
convertMp4ToAviFaq2Q: Is AVI better quality than MP4?
convertMp4ToAviFaq2A: AVI is a container format, not a codec, so quality depends on the codec used inside it. Modern MP4 with H.264 typically delivers better compression efficiency than common AVI codecs. AVI files are usually larger at equivalent quality because they often use less efficient compression methods.
convertMp4ToAviFaq3Q: Will the AVI file be larger than the MP4?
convertMp4ToAviFaq3A: Yes, AVI files are generally larger than MP4 because the AVI container and its typical codecs are less efficient at compression. Expect the AVI output to be 50-200% larger depending on the codec and settings used. This is the trade-off for broader compatibility with legacy systems.
convertWebmToMp4HowTo: How to Convert WebM to MP4
convertWebmToMp4Step1Desc: Select your WebM video file. Common for videos downloaded from the web or recorded by browsers.
convertWebmToMp4Step2Desc: Choose MP4 as output format with H.264 encoding for maximum device and platform compatibility.
convertWebmToMp4Step3Desc: Save your MP4 video. It can now be played on virtually every device, TV, phone, and media player.
convertWebmToMp4Faq1A: MP4 is the most universally compatible video format, supported by all smartphones, smart TVs, media players, and social media platforms. WebM has limited support on Apple devices and some older systems. Converting to MP4 ensures your video plays everywhere without compatibility issues.
convertWebmToMp4Faq2Q: Can I play the converted MP4 on iPhone?
convertWebmToMp4Faq2A: Yes, MP4 with H.264 encoding is natively supported on all iPhones, iPads, and Apple devices. After conversion, you can play the video directly in the Photos app, share it via iMessage or AirDrop, and upload it to any social media platform without additional apps.
convertWebmToMp4Faq3Q: Is there quality loss when converting WebM to MP4?
convertWebmToMp4Faq3A: There is a small quality reduction because the video is re-encoded from one lossy codec to another. At high quality settings, this difference is barely perceptible. For best results, keep the output resolution and bitrate close to the original WebM file specifications.
convertMp4ToGifHowTo: How to Convert MP4 to GIF
convertMp4ToGifStep1Desc: Select the MP4 video clip you want to turn into an animated GIF. Shorter clips produce better results.
convertMp4ToGifStep2Desc: Set frame rate, resolution, and trim the clip length. Lower frame rates and smaller dimensions create smaller GIF files.
convertMp4ToGifStep3Desc: Save your animated GIF. Share it on social media, messaging apps, forums, or embed it in websites and emails.
convertMp4ToGifFaq1Q: What is the ideal length for a GIF?
convertMp4ToGifFaq1A: Most effective GIFs are 3-6 seconds long. GIF files grow rapidly with duration because every frame is stored as a full image. A 10-second GIF at 15 FPS can easily exceed 10MB. Keep GIFs short and focused on the key moment for the best balance of quality and file size.
convertMp4ToGifFaq2Q: Why is my GIF file so large?
convertMp4ToGifFaq2A: GIF format uses lossless frame-by-frame storage with a 256-color palette, which is inherently inefficient for video content. Reduce file size by lowering the resolution to 480px width or less, reducing frame rate to 10-12 FPS, and trimming the clip to the shortest possible duration.
convertMp4ToGifFaq3Q: How can I make a higher quality GIF?
convertMp4ToGifFaq3A: Start with a high-quality source video with good lighting and stable footage. Use a higher frame rate like 15-20 FPS for smoother animation. Keep the resolution reasonable at 640px width maximum. Shorter clips allow more frames per second within a reasonable file size limit.
compressJpgStep1Desc: Drag and drop or select one or more JPG/JPEG images. Batch compression is supported for multiple files at once.
compressJpgStep2Desc: Set your desired quality level. Lower quality means smaller file sizes. Preview the result before downloading.
compressJpgStep3Desc: Save your optimized JPG images with dramatically reduced file sizes while maintaining visual quality.
compressJpgFaq1Q: How much can JPG compression reduce file size?
compressJpgFaq1A: JPG compression can reduce file sizes by 50-80% depending on quality settings and image content. A 5MB photo can typically be compressed to 500KB-1MB without noticeable quality loss. Images with large uniform areas compress more efficiently than highly detailed textures.
compressJpgFaq2Q: Does compressing a JPG reduce image dimensions?
compressJpgFaq2A: No, compression adjusts only the encoding quality without changing pixel dimensions. Your 4000x3000 image remains 4000x3000 pixels after compression. The file size reduction comes from how the color data is stored, not from reducing resolution. Resizing dimensions is a separate operation.
compressJpgFaq3Q: Can I compress a JPG multiple times?
compressJpgFaq3A: You can, but each round of JPG compression introduces additional quality loss because JPG is lossy. Re-compressing an already compressed JPG degrades quality further while yielding diminishing size reductions. For best results, always compress from the original high-quality source file.
compressPngStep1Desc: Select your PNG images by dragging them in or browsing. Works with all PNG files including transparent ones.
compressPngStep2Desc: Choose your compression level. The tool optimizes PNG encoding while preserving transparency and visual quality.
compressPngStep3Desc: Save your compressed PNG files. Transparency is fully preserved with significantly reduced file sizes.
compressPngFaq1A: Yes, PNG compression fully preserves the alpha channel and all transparency data. The compression works by optimizing how pixel data is encoded and filtered without removing transparency information. Your logos, icons, and overlay graphics will maintain their transparent backgrounds perfectly.
compressPngFaq2Q: How is PNG compression different from JPG compression?
compressPngFaq2A: PNG uses lossless compression by default, meaning no pixel data is discarded. JPG uses lossy compression that permanently removes data to achieve smaller sizes. PNG compression works by finding more efficient ways to encode the same data, while JPG compression works by discarding visual details.
compressPngFaq3Q: Why are PNG files larger than JPG files?
compressPngFaq3A: PNG preserves every pixel exactly through lossless compression, which limits how small files can get. JPG achieves smaller sizes by discarding visual information humans typically cannot notice. For photographs, JPG is more efficient. For graphics, logos, and screenshots with sharp edges, PNG is preferred.
compressWebpStep1Desc: Select your WebP files by dragging and dropping or browsing. Both lossy and lossless WebP files are supported.
compressWebpStep2Desc: Fine-tune the compression level to balance file size and visual quality for your specific use case.
compressWebpStep3Desc: Save your compressed WebP images. Further reduce already-efficient WebP files for maximum web performance.
compressWebpFaq1Q: Can WebP files be compressed further?
compressWebpFaq1A: Yes, especially WebP files exported at high quality settings. Re-compressing at a slightly lower quality level can reduce file size by 20-50% with minimal visual impact. This is useful for optimizing WebP images exported from design tools at unnecessarily high quality settings.
compressWebpFaq2Q: What quality setting is best for web images?
compressWebpFaq2A: For most web images, a WebP quality setting of 75-85% provides excellent visual quality with optimal file size. Hero images and product photos may benefit from 85-90%. Thumbnails and decorative elements can use 60-75%. Always preview compressed results to verify acceptable quality for your use case.
compressWebpFaq3Q: Is WebP better than JPG for website performance?
compressWebpFaq3A: WebP consistently delivers 25-35% smaller files than JPG at equivalent visual quality, directly improving page load speed and Core Web Vitals scores. Google recommends WebP for web images, and all major browsers now support it. The smaller files also reduce hosting bandwidth costs significantly.
compressMp4Step1Desc: Select your MP4 video file. The tool runs entirely in your browser, so no video data is uploaded to any server.
compressMp4Step2Desc: Adjust resolution, bitrate, and quality. Lower resolution and bitrate produce smaller files. Preview estimated output size.
compressMp4Step3Desc: Save your compressed MP4 with reduced file size. Share it via email, messaging apps, or upload to social media.
compressMp4Faq1Q: How much can MP4 video be compressed?
compressMp4Faq1A: MP4 compression can reduce video file size by 40-70% depending on original encoding, resolution, and target quality. A 100MB video can often be reduced to 30-50MB while maintaining watchable quality. Videos originally recorded at very high bitrates see the largest reductions.
compressMp4Faq2A: Not necessarily. You can compress video by reducing bitrate alone while keeping the original resolution. However, reducing resolution from 1080p to 720p is an effective way to dramatically cut file size. On mobile screens, the visual difference between 720p and 1080p is often imperceptible.
compressMp4Faq3Q: What is the best resolution for sharing videos online?
compressMp4Faq3A: 720p (1280x720) is the sweet spot for sharing via email and messaging apps, offering good quality at manageable file sizes. For social media uploads, 1080p is recommended since platforms apply their own compression. For WhatsApp and similar apps with size limits, 480p keeps files under 16MB.
resizeInstagramHowTo: How to Resize Images for Instagram
resizeInstagramStep1Desc: Select the image you want to resize for Instagram. Supports JPG, PNG, WebP, and other common formats.
resizeInstagramStep2Desc: Select the target size: 1080x1080 for posts, 1080x1350 for portrait, or 1080x1920 for stories and reels.
resizeInstagramStep3Desc: Save your perfectly sized Instagram image. Upload it directly to Instagram without platform cropping issues.
resizeInstagramFaq1Q: What is the best image size for Instagram posts?
resizeInstagramFaq1A: Instagram supports 1080x1080 (square), 1080x1350 (portrait 4:5), and 1080x566 (landscape). Portrait 1080x1350 takes up the most feed space and gets the highest engagement. Always upload at 1080px width minimum to prevent Instagram from displaying a blurry, upscaled version.
resizeInstagramFaq2Q: What size should Instagram stories be?
resizeInstagramFaq2A: Instagram Stories and Reels should be 1080x1920 pixels at a 9:16 aspect ratio for full-screen display. Leave 250 pixels of safe margin at the top and bottom to avoid your content being covered by the username bar and reply area on viewers' screens.
resizeInstagramFaq3A: Yes, Instagram compresses all uploaded images to reduce server costs. To minimize quality loss, upload images at exactly the recommended dimensions in JPG format at 95% quality. Oversized images undergo more aggressive compression, so matching the exact target resolution produces the sharpest results.
resizeFacebookHowTo: How to Resize Images for Facebook
resizeFacebookStep1Desc: Select the photo you want to resize for Facebook. Works with all common image formats.
resizeFacebookStep2Desc: Choose the target: 820x312 for cover photo, 1200x630 for shared posts, or 170x170 for profile picture.
resizeFacebookStep3Desc: Save your resized image at the exact Facebook dimensions. Upload without cropping or quality degradation.
resizeFacebookFaq1Q: What is the best size for Facebook cover photos?
resizeFacebookFaq1A: Facebook cover photos display at 820x312 pixels on desktop and 640x360 pixels on mobile. Design at 820x462 to accommodate both views, centering important content in the middle area. Use PNG format for covers with text or logos, and JPG for photographic covers.
resizeFacebookFaq2Q: What dimensions work best for Facebook shared images?
resizeFacebookFaq2A: For link preview images and shared posts, use 1200x630 pixels (1.91:1 ratio). This is also the Open Graph standard that displays correctly when your page is shared. For photo uploads, 2048x2048 pixels maximum resolution is supported, but 1200px width is optimal for news feed display.
resizeFacebookFaq3Q: Does Facebook reduce image quality when uploading?
resizeFacebookFaq3A: Yes, Facebook compresses uploaded images significantly. To minimize quality loss, upload JPG files under 100KB for profile photos and under 1MB for posts. Use sRGB color space and match exact recommended dimensions. Facebook applies less compression to images that already meet its target specifications.
resizeTwitterHowTo: How to Resize Images for Twitter/X
resizeTwitterStep1Desc: Select the image you want to optimize for Twitter/X. All common image formats are supported.
resizeTwitterStep2Desc: Select the target: 1500x500 for header, 1600x900 for in-stream images, or 400x400 for profile photo.
resizeTwitterStep3Desc: Save your Twitter-optimized image. It will display perfectly in timelines without unexpected cropping.
resizeTwitterFaq1Q: What is the best image size for Twitter/X posts?
resizeTwitterFaq1A: Twitter recommends 1600x900 pixels (16:9 ratio) for single-image tweets that display fully without cropping. For two images, use 700x800 each. Twitter supports up to 5MB for photos and 15MB for GIFs. Images wider than 4096 pixels are automatically downsized by the platform.
resizeTwitterFaq2Q: What are the Twitter/X header dimensions?
resizeTwitterFaq2A: Twitter headers should be 1500x500 pixels (3:1 ratio). Keep important content centered because the edges may be cropped on different devices and screen sizes. The profile photo overlaps the lower-left area of the header on desktop, so avoid placing key elements there.
resizeTwitterFaq3Q: How does Twitter crop images in the timeline?
resizeTwitterFaq3A: Twitter uses an AI-based smart crop algorithm that attempts to find the most visually interesting focal point. To avoid unexpected cropping, use the recommended 16:9 aspect ratio for single images. Center your subject and keep important elements away from edges to ensure they remain visible after auto-cropping.
resizeYoutubeStep1Desc: Select the image you want to use as a YouTube thumbnail. High-resolution source images produce the best results.
resizeYoutubeStep2Desc: Resize and crop your image to the exact YouTube thumbnail dimensions of 1280x720 pixels at 16:9 aspect ratio.
resizeYoutubeStep3Desc: Save your YouTube-ready thumbnail. Upload it directly in YouTube Studio when publishing or editing your video.
resizeYoutubeFaq1Q: What is the ideal YouTube thumbnail size?
resizeYoutubeFaq1A: YouTube thumbnails should be exactly 1280x720 pixels with a 16:9 aspect ratio. The minimum width is 640 pixels, and the maximum file size is 2MB. JPG, PNG, and GIF formats are accepted. Always design at the full 1280x720 resolution for the sharpest display across all devices.
resizeYoutubeFaq2Q: What makes a YouTube thumbnail get more clicks?
resizeYoutubeFaq2A: High-CTR thumbnails use bright contrasting colors, large readable text (3-5 words maximum), expressive human faces, and a clear focal point. Avoid clutter and small details that become invisible at small sizes. Test your thumbnail at 120x67 pixels to verify it remains compelling and readable.
resizeYoutubeFaq3Q: Should YouTube thumbnails be JPG or PNG?
resizeYoutubeFaq3A: JPG is preferred for photographic thumbnails because it keeps files well under the 2MB limit. PNG is better for thumbnails with text overlays, logos, and graphic elements where sharp edges matter. If your PNG exceeds 2MB, compress it or switch to JPG at 90-95% quality.
removeBgStep1Desc: Select the photo you want to remove the background from. Works best with clear subjects and good contrast.
removeBgStep2Desc: The AI automatically detects the subject and removes the background. Processing happens entirely in your browser.
removeBgStep3Desc: Save your image with a transparent background as a PNG file. Ready for compositing, presentations, or product listings.
removeBgFaq1Q: How does AI background removal work?
removeBgFaq1A: The AI model analyzes your image to identify the main subject using semantic segmentation. It classifies each pixel as foreground or background, then removes background pixels to create transparency. Modern AI models handle complex edges like hair and fur with impressive accuracy.
removeBgFaq2Q: What types of images work best for background removal?
removeBgFaq2A: Images with clear contrast between subject and background produce the best results. Well-lit photos with a single main subject work ideally. Complex scenes with multiple overlapping subjects, very similar foreground and background colors, or transparent objects like glass may require manual touch-up.
removeBgFaq3Q: Is my image uploaded to a server for processing?
removeBgFaq3A: No, the entire background removal process runs locally in your browser using a WebAssembly AI model. Your images never leave your device, ensuring complete privacy. This is especially important for sensitive images like confidential product photos or personal portraits.
addWatermarkHowTo: How to Add Watermark to Photos
addWatermarkStep1Desc: Select one or more images you want to watermark. Batch processing lets you watermark multiple photos at once.
addWatermarkStep2Desc: Add text or upload a logo. Adjust position, size, opacity, and rotation to create your ideal watermark placement.
addWatermarkStep3Desc: Save your protected photos with the watermark applied. Original unwatermarked files remain private on your device.
addWatermarkFaq1Q: What is the best watermark opacity setting?
addWatermarkFaq1A: An opacity of 30-50% strikes the ideal balance between visibility and subtlety. At 30%, the watermark is noticeable without distracting from the image content. At 50%, it provides stronger protection against unauthorized use. Test different levels to find what works for your specific images and branding needs.
addWatermarkFaq2Q: Where should I place a watermark on a photo?
addWatermarkFaq2A: The lower-right corner is the most common placement, but it is also the easiest to crop out. For better protection, position watermarks in the center or use a diagonal repeating pattern across the entire image. The ideal placement covers important content areas that would be ruined if the watermark were removed.
addWatermarkFaq3Q: Can watermarks be removed from photos?
addWatermarkFaq3A: Semi-transparent watermarks can be partially removed using AI tools, but a well-placed watermark across detailed areas makes clean removal very difficult. Use repeating tile patterns, position watermarks over key details, and combine text with logo elements to create watermarks that resist automated removal attempts.
mergePhotosStep1Desc: Select two or more photos you want to combine. Supports JPG, PNG, WebP and other common image formats.
mergePhotosStep2Desc: Choose horizontal, vertical, or grid layout. Adjust spacing, alignment, and image order by dragging to rearrange.
mergePhotosStep3Desc: Save your merged photo as a single image file. Perfect for comparisons, collages, and social media posts.
mergePhotosFaq1Q: How many photos can I merge at once?
mergePhotosFaq1A: You can merge as many photos as your browser memory allows, typically 10-20 high-resolution images without issues. For very large batches, the tool may slow down depending on your device capabilities. The output image dimensions grow with each added photo, so consider your final use case when adding many images.
mergePhotosFaq2Q: Can I merge photos of different sizes?
mergePhotosFaq2A: Yes, the tool automatically handles images of different dimensions. You can choose to resize all images to match the largest or smallest dimension, or maintain original proportions with padding. Horizontal layouts align images vertically, while vertical layouts align them horizontally for a clean result.
mergePhotosFaq3Q: What is the best layout for before-and-after comparisons?
mergePhotosFaq3A: Horizontal side-by-side layout works best for before-and-after comparisons, placing the original on the left and the result on the right. Ensure both images are the same height for a clean join. Add minimal spacing between images so viewers can easily compare details across the boundary.
extractMp3HowTo: How to Extract MP3 from Video
extractMp3Step1Desc: Select your video file in MP4, WebM, AVI, MOV, or other common format. The audio track will be extracted.
extractMp3Step2Desc: The tool extracts the audio stream from your video and converts it to MP3 format using browser-based FFmpeg.
extractMp3Step3Desc: Save the extracted MP3 audio file. Use it as a podcast, music track, voice recording, or audio backup.
extractMp3Faq1Q: What audio quality can I expect from extracted MP3?
extractMp3Faq1A: The MP3 quality depends on the original video's audio track. Most videos contain audio at 128-256 kbps. The extraction preserves this quality and re-encodes it as MP3 at your chosen bitrate. For music, use 256-320 kbps. For speech and podcasts, 128 kbps provides clear quality at smaller file sizes.
extractMp3Faq2Q: Can I extract audio from YouTube videos?
extractMp3Faq2A: This tool extracts audio from video files stored on your device. You would first need to have the video file saved locally. The tool does not download videos from URLs or streaming platforms. Once you have a local video file, you can extract its audio track to MP3 instantly.
extractMp3Faq3Q: Is the video uploaded to a server during extraction?
extractMp3Faq3A: No, the entire extraction process runs locally in your browser using WebAssembly-powered FFmpeg. Your video file never leaves your device, ensuring complete privacy. This is particularly important for extracting audio from private recordings, confidential meetings, or personal video content.
compressGptImage2: Compress GPT Image 2 Output Online - Free
compressGptImage2Desc: Compress GPT Image 2 PNG output from OpenAI's new image model. Shrink 4K output from 12MB down to under 300KB without visible quality loss.
compressGptImage2HowTo: How to Compress GPT Image 2 Output
compressGptImage2Step1Desc: Drag and drop the PNG or JPG you generated with OpenAI's GPT Image 2 or ChatGPT. Files up to 50MB are supported.
compressGptImage2Step2Desc: Set quality between 75-90%. GPT Image 2 output compresses well — 82% usually looks identical to the OpenAI original.
compressGptImage2Step3Desc: Save your optimized file. A 12MB GPT Image 2 4K PNG from OpenAI typically shrinks to under 300KB.
compressGptImage2Faq1Q: Why is GPT Image 2 output from OpenAI so large?
compressGptImage2Faq1A: GPT Image 2 generates at native 2048x2048 or 4096x4096 resolution and exports as uncompressed PNG by default, producing files of 8-15MB per image. For web pages, email, and social media you'll want to compress GPT Image 2 output down to the 200-500KB range without losing visible quality.
compressGptImage2Faq2Q: Does compressing break OpenAI's C2PA watermark metadata?
compressGptImage2Faq2A: OpenAI embeds C2PA provenance metadata in GPT Image 2 outputs. JPEG compression usually preserves this metadata. If you need to strip metadata entirely, use the image converter tool to re-encode to a new format, but note that this removes the authenticity signature OpenAI attaches to GPT Image 2 renders.
compressGptImage2Faq3Q: Is my GPT Image 2 output uploaded to a server?
compressGptImage2Faq3A: No. All compression runs locally in your browser using WebAssembly. Your OpenAI GPT Image 2 outputs never leave your device, which matters if the prompt included confidential references, unreleased products, or proprietary branding.
resizeAiImage: Resize AI Image Online — GPT Image 2, Midjourney, DALL-E
resizeAiImageDesc: Resize AI-generated images from GPT Image 2, ChatGPT, DALL-E, Midjourney, or Nano Banana to Instagram, Twitter, Facebook, YouTube dimensions.
resizeAiImageStep1Desc: Drop in any AI-generated PNG or JPG from GPT Image 2, OpenAI ChatGPT, Midjourney, DALL-E, or Nano Banana Pro.
resizeAiImageStep2Desc: Pick a social media preset — 1080x1080 Instagram, 1200x630 OG card, 1500x500 X/Twitter header — or set custom dimensions.
resizeAiImageStep3Desc: Crop and download. Upload the result directly to your target platform with no cropping surprises.
resizeAiImageFaq1Q: Will resizing lose quality on AI-generated images?
resizeAiImageFaq1A: Downscaling from 2K or 4K GPT Image 2 output to 1080px is effectively lossless — you're discarding pixels the target platform would throw away anyway. Upscaling from small output is where quality suffers, so always generate GPT Image 2 renders at the highest resolution OpenAI supports.
resizeAiImageFaq2Q: What size should I render in GPT Image 2 for Instagram?
resizeAiImageFaq2A: Generate at 2048x2048 in GPT Image 2 then crop to 1080x1080 for Instagram posts, 1080x1350 for 4:5 portrait posts, or 1080x1920 for Stories and Reels. The extra pixels give you cropping flexibility without regenerating in OpenAI.
resizeAiImageFaq3Q: Does this work for Midjourney and DALL-E outputs too?
resizeAiImageFaq3A: Yes. The resize tool handles any image regardless of source. PNG from GPT Image 2, WebP from Nano Banana Pro, JPG from Midjourney — all work identically. Your original file is not sent anywhere.
watermarkAiImage: Watermark AI-Generated Image Online - Free for GPT Image 2 & Midjourney
watermarkAiImageDesc: Add custom text or logo watermarks to AI-generated images from GPT Image 2, OpenAI DALL-E, Midjourney or Nano Banana. Protect and disclose your AI artwork before sharing.
watermarkAiImageStep1Desc: Drop in your PNG or JPG from GPT Image 2, OpenAI ChatGPT, Midjourney, or any AI image generator.
watermarkAiImageStep2Desc: Type your watermark text or upload a logo. Set opacity, position, font size, and color for a professional finish.
watermarkAiImageStep3Desc: Save your protected image. Share it on social media or portfolios while keeping attribution clear.
watermarkAiImageFaq1Q: Do I need to watermark GPT Image 2 outputs?
watermarkAiImageFaq1A: Ethically and often legally, yes. Platforms like X, Instagram, and Meta properties increasingly require disclosure when content is AI-generated. A small '#AI' or 'Generated with GPT Image 2' watermark on your OpenAI outputs keeps you compliant and transparent with your audience.
watermarkAiImageFaq2Q: Will watermarking affect C2PA metadata from OpenAI?
watermarkAiImageFaq2A: Editing pixels breaks C2PA signatures because the file hash changes. This is expected behavior. If you need to preserve OpenAI's provenance signatures on GPT Image 2 output, keep a separate archival copy with the original C2PA intact.
watermarkAiImageFaq3Q: Can I watermark multiple AI images at once?
watermarkAiImageFaq3A: Yes. Batch watermarking is supported. Upload a folder of GPT Image 2 or Midjourney outputs, configure the watermark once, and apply it across the entire batch. Processing runs locally in your browser.
removeBgAiImage: Remove Background From AI-Generated Image - Free Online Tool
removeBgAiImageDesc: Remove backgrounds from OpenAI GPT Image 2, DALL-E, Midjourney, or ChatGPT outputs with one click. AI-powered, runs in your browser. Perfect transparent PNG result.
removeBgAiImageHowTo: How to Remove Background From AI Images
removeBgAiImageStep1Desc: Drop in your AI-generated image from GPT Image 2, OpenAI ChatGPT, Midjourney, or any image generator.
removeBgAiImageStep2Desc: Our client-side AI detects the main subject and removes the background automatically in 2-5 seconds.
removeBgAiImageStep3Desc: Save your cutout as a transparent PNG, ready to overlay on any background or drop into a product mockup.
removeBgAiImageFaq1Q: Does background removal work well on GPT Image 2 output?
removeBgAiImageFaq1A: Yes — GPT Image 2's clean, well-lit outputs are ideal for background removal. OpenAI's model generates subjects with crisp edges and good subject-background contrast, which makes the AI cutout even more accurate than on photo-realistic photos.
removeBgAiImageFaq2Q: Can I remove background from complex AI scenes?
removeBgAiImageFaq2A: For scenes with multiple subjects or intricate backgrounds, the AI extracts the most prominent foreground subject. If GPT Image 2 generated a portrait with a complex background, removal works well. For multi-subject scenes, re-prompt in OpenAI with a simpler background first.
removeBgAiImageFaq3Q: Is the image uploaded to a server?
removeBgAiImageFaq3A: No. Background removal runs entirely in your browser using client-side AI. Your GPT Image 2 outputs and any confidential prompts embedded in metadata never leave your device.
convertGptImageToJpg: Convert GPT Image 2 PNG to JPG Online - Free
convertGptImageToJpgDesc: Convert OpenAI GPT Image 2, DALL-E, or ChatGPT PNG outputs to JPG format. Shrink file size by 80% and maximize device compatibility.
convertGptImageToJpgHowTo: How to Convert GPT Image 2 PNG to JPG
convertGptImageToJpgStep1Desc: Drop in the PNG file generated by OpenAI GPT Image 2, ChatGPT, or the OpenAI Image API.
convertGptImageToJpgStep2Desc: Choose JPG as the output format and set quality between 85-95%. Higher quality preserves detail; 85% halves the file size.
convertGptImageToJpgStep3Desc: Save your converted JPG. A 12MB GPT Image 2 PNG from OpenAI typically converts to a 1-2MB JPG at visually identical quality.
convertGptImageToJpgFaq1Q: Why convert GPT Image 2 PNG from OpenAI to JPG?
convertGptImageToJpgFaq1A: PNG is lossless but produces huge files. GPT Image 2's native PNG output from OpenAI lands at 8-15MB. JPG compresses photographic content to 10-20% of PNG size with no visible quality loss. For web, email, and social, JPG is the practical choice.
convertGptImageToJpgFaq2A: JPG uses lossy compression, so there's technically some quality loss. But at 90% quality, the difference is invisible to the human eye on photographic content like GPT Image 2 output. For graphics with sharp edges or transparency, stick with PNG.
convertGptImageToJpgFaq3Q: Will I lose transparency converting to JPG?
convertGptImageToJpgFaq3A: Yes — JPG does not support transparency. If your OpenAI GPT Image 2 output has a transparent background, JPG will fill it with white. Use WebP or stick with PNG for transparent cutouts.
